background: Determine the sample size required for conducting a two-sided test of H0: p1 = p2 with an equal number of subjects in each group. The test parameters are α = 0.05 (z_{1-(.05/2)} = 1.96), 1-β = 0.90 (z_{.90} = 1.282), p1 = 0.10, and p2 = 0.05. Additionally, consider the case where a continuity correction is incorporated into the test statistic.

task.query: Determine the sample size required for conducting a two-sided test of H0: p1 = p2 with the given parameters (α = 0.05, 1-β = 0.90, p1 = 0.10, p2 = 0.05).
How many individuals should be studied if we want to incorporate a continuity correction into our test statistic?
task.answer:
$$
\text { Solution: } \begin{aligned}
n^{\prime} & =\frac{n}{4}\left(1+\sqrt{1+\frac{4}{n\left|p_1-p_2\right|}}\right)^2 \
& =\frac{581.26}{4}\left(1+\sqrt{1+\frac{4}{581.26|0.10-0.05|}}\right)^2 \
& =620.6
\end{aligned}
$$

Therefore, resolve to study 621 observations per group.
